title: When sending an email with multiple monday custom board email addresses (@*.monday.com) in recipients, only one board receives the item while others are ignored. This used to work previously but has become inconsistent in the last few days
excerpt: When sending an email with multiple monday custom board email addresses (@*.monday.com) in recipients, only one board receives the item while others are ignored. This used to work previously but has become inconsistent i…

posts.author: Hashir (Hashir)
posts.cookedHtml: When sending an email with multiple monday custom board email addresses (@*.monday.com) in recipients, only one board receives the item while others are ignored. This used to work previously but has become inconsistent in the last few days.

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Interesting… a couple things I’d want to clarify before assuming it’s broken:

  • Are you putting the monday board emails in To, CC, or BCC?

  • Is it always the same board that receives it, or does it vary?

  • Are the emails being sent from the same sender each time?

  • Did anything change recently with email integrations, permissions, or domains?

  • Roughly how many board email addresses are you including at once?

posts.author: drtanvisachar (Dr. Tanvi Sachar- Certified Monday.com Partner)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey @Hashir !

Only one board email is processed per incoming email. Multiple monday board email addresses in the same email are not reliably supported.

Send separate emails to each board’s email address instead of including multiple in one message. Make sure each email has only one @*.monday.com recipient.

This behavior can appear inconsistent, but the system only guarantees processing for a single board per email.

If you’d like hands-on help or want us to walk through this live, you can book a 1:1 paid 60-minute strategy session with our team here:
Calendly

posts.author: Hashir (Hashir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Dr.Tanvi, thank you for your reply.

I understand this, however, my only concern is that this used to work perfectly a week ago. We have been adding multiple monday email IDs in one email in bcc for a long time and it used to create items in the individual boards every time.

posts.author: Hashir (Hashir)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi @ProjectMastermind Thank you for your reply. To answer your questions

  1. We usually put the monday board emails in bcc. but the behaviour is same even if they are in to or cc
  2. Boards can vary, we have 32 team members and 32 individual boards
  3. No senders can vary as well, but all the senders have been added as users in monday administration
  4. No nothing changed recently.
  5. two to three at once

posts.author: ProjectMastermind (IngenuityKat)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hashir:

When sending an email with multiple monday custom board email addresses (@*.monday.com) in recipients, only one board receives the item while others are ignored. This used to work previously but has become inconsistent in the last few days.

I would definitely submit this as a monday support ticket with all that detail as it sounds like a bug.
